# komodo_terminal (Data Source)

Reads an existing Komodo terminal session on a target resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up a Server terminal by name
data "komodo_terminal" "server" {
  target_type = "Server"
  target_id   = "69db2f6e0816ddac8244a5b3"
  name        = "my-terminal"
}

# Look up a Container terminal by name
data "komodo_terminal" "container" {
  target_type = "Container"
  target_id   = "69db2f6e0816ddac8244a5b3" # the server hosting the container
  container   = "adguard"
  name        = "my-container-terminal"
}

# Look up a Stack service terminal by name
data "komodo_terminal" "stack" {
  target_type = "Stack"
  target_id   = "69dba72e0816ddac8244ae18"
  service     = "nginx"
  name        = "my-stack-terminal"
}

# Look up a Deployment terminal by name
data "komodo_terminal" "deployment" {
  target_type = "Deployment"
  target_id   = "69dba72e0816ddac8244ae18"
  name        = "my-deployment-terminal"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the terminal session to look up.
- `target_id` (String) The primary target ID or name. For `Server`: the server. For `Container`: the server hosting the container. For `Stack`: the stack. For `Deployment`: the deployment.
- `target_type` (String) The type of target to look up terminals on. One of `Server`, `Container`, `Stack`, `Deployment`.

### Optional

- `container` (String) The container name. Only used when `target_type` is `Container`.
- `service` (String) The stack service name. Only used when `target_type` is `Stack`.

### Read-Only

- `command` (String) The shell command used to initialise the terminal.
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp in RFC3339 format.
- `stored_size_kb` (Number) The size of stored terminal output in kilobytes.
